fixes.description: Flip BZR1 -> TB1 edge from -1 (inhibition) to +1 (activation)
fixes.reason: T114 (BZR1 KD), T115 (BR treatment), T161 (osbzr1 d53-D) all predict opposite-sign at the phenotype level. Phenotype-level evidence (BR reduces tillers) requires BZR1 to net-activate TB1.
fixes.biological_justification: Observation: OsBZR1-D53 complex stabilised by BR reduces tiller outgrowth (Fang 2020). Since TB1 canonically represses tillering (TB1->Tiller_Number -1), the observed phenotype requires BZR1 to activate TB1 transcription. The original "represses OsTB1" wording in the paper is inconsistent with its own tiller phenotype data; the phenotype-level evidence is used to set the edge sign.

fixes.description: Add RR21 -> TB1 (-1) closing the HK4->AHP->RR21 branch to TB1
fixes.reason: T038 (OsAHP KD) fails because HK4->AHP->RR21 branch has no direct TB1 output after iter 1. Adding RR21->TB1 -1 parallels the direct HK4->TB1 -1 we added previously and carries the AHP-knockdown signal all the way to OsTB1.
fixes.biological_justification: OsRR21 is the canonical type-B response regulator downstream of OsHK4 phosphorelay (Chun 2023) and participates in axillary-meristem transcriptional regulation of OsTB1. osrr21 single mutants phenocopy ohk4 in tillering. The edge formalises the B-type RR -> OsTB1 repression branch of CK signalling.
